payload.answer: The proposed update should not be committed as-is. Please choose a repair approach for the large-table atomization issue.
citations: Role contract: Generate repair/user-choice options only; do not decide, commit, append, merge, overwrite, supersede, or mark anything as saved.
Input: For context, LLM proposes 500 memory cards, one per CSV row.
repair_options: Keep the CSV data as a single grouped memory card instead of atomizing into 500 separate cards.
Split only the most important rows into a small selected subset of cards, and leave the rest uncommitted.
Add the rows separately as distinct cards without merging them, if the user wants full granularity.
Ask the user which CSV rows should be preserved before generating any memory cards.
Batch the rows into a few thematic cards rather than one card per row.
